diagrams.title: Three Schema Architecture
diagrams.description: A layered diagram showing the three levels of database architecture: External (User View), Conceptual (Logical), and Internal (Physical). Arrows show the mappings between levels, illustrating how data is transformed from user views to physical storage.
elements: External Schema Layer (top) - multiple user views/forms
Conceptual Schema Layer (middle) - logical database structure
Internal Schema Layer (bottom) - physical storage details
Mapping arrows between External and Conceptual
Mapping arrows between Conceptual and Internal
diagrams.purpose: Explains the three-level architecture foundation of data independence, showing where each schema sits and how they relate to each other.

diagrams.title: Data Independence Types
diagrams.description: A vertical layer diagram showing the two types of data independence with arrows indicating what changes can occur without affecting other levels. Shows physical data independence (between Internal and Conceptual) and logical data independence (between Conceptual and External).
elements: Physical Data Independence arrow (Internal to Conceptual)
Logical Data Independence arrow (Conceptual to External)
Change indicators showing what modifications are protected
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the concept of data independence - the ability to change one schema without affecting the next higher level, with clear distinction between physical and logical independence.
